response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack/web engineer with strong software craftsmanship, but the role requires deep hardware-adjacent expertise: SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no experience in silicon modeling, deep-learning workload optimization, or computer architecture. Brief C/C++ work at National Instruments and low-latency DLang services are the closest touchpoints, but they do not approach principal-level performance architecture for AI hardware.
strengths: Strong low-latency systems experience (DLang, async I/O, high-throughput microservices)
Broad engineering versatility across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Solid engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and teaching/mentoring
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or AI accelerator background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, far from principal-level performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Low-latency systems and performance tuning experience
